content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN. You can perform the following operation on it any number of times (possibly, zero):


Choose any two distinct indices iii and jjj (1≤i,j≤N1\leq i , j \leq N1≤i,j≤N and i≠ji \neq ji=j), and multiply both AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ by −1-1−1.

That is, set AiA_iAi​ to −Ai-A_i−Ai​, and also AjA_jAj​ to −Aj-A_j−Aj​.
Find the **maximum** possible sum of the array AAA after performing this operation several times.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN — the number of elements in AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible sum of AAA after several operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4⋅1054\cdot 10^54⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The sum of the array is 777, which is the best we can do. No operations are needed.
Test case 222: Consider the following operation:

Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=5j = 5j=5. The array becomes [3,4,6,2,1][3, 4, 6, 2, 1][3,4,6,2,1].
Its sum is now 161616, which is the maximum possible.
